mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, consumer spending growth decelerates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of instances where the unemployment rate exceeds the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of occurrences over a five-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect accelerating factory output and supply chain utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns show no statistical significance during early-cycle phases or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent federal funds rate hikes and rising unemployment indicate monetary tightening outpacing labor market resilience, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns with greater than 60 percent accuracy in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 50 percent of quarters where GDP growth surpasses the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns exhibit a negative mean or Rank IC below 0.01 across all payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with suppressed short-term rates compresses real yields and signals delayed policy tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive forward returns in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances or shows no predictive power in inflationary regimes.
